WEST HOLLYWOOD HEALTHCARE & WELLNESS CENTRE, LP

855 NORTH FAIRFAX AVENUE, LOS ANGELES, CA 90046

Setting
Urban
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.43 4.36 3.92 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.56 0.76 0.49 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.16 0.97 1.02 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.71 2.63 2.40 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.72 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.07 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.90
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.41
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.45
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
29.8%
annual
RN turnover
36.4%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.55
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.12
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-06-13 10 10 0 60 1 60
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-05-28 31 28 3 180 1 180
Total weighted health score
90.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (55)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
53 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-06-13 2025-07-03
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-06-13 2025-07-03
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2025-06-13 2025-07-03
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-06-13 2025-07-03
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-06-13 2025-07-03
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-06-13 2025-07-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-06-13 2025-07-03
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2025-06-13 2025-07-03
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. E 2025-06-13 2025-07-03
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2025-06-13 2025-07-03
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0646 Notify the appropriate authorities when residents with MD or ID services has a significant change in condition. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0808 Ensure therapeutic diets are prescribed by the attending physician and may be delegated to a registered or licensed dietitian, to the extent allowed by State law. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. D 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2024-05-28 2024-06-28
0573 Let each resident or the resident's legal representative access or purchase copies of all the resident's records. D 2023-04-18 2023-05-16
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2023-04-11 2023-04-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-04-11 2023-04-25
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2022-05-13 2022-06-30
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2022-05-13 2022-06-30
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2022-05-13 2022-06-30
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2022-05-13 2022-06-30
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2022-05-13 2022-06-30
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-05-13 2022-06-30
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2022-05-13 2022-06-30
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2022-05-13 2022-06-30
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2022-05-13 2022-06-30
